findings.title: SSH port exposed on development host
findings.description: SSH (port 22) is accessible from the internet on the development host. Development environments often have weaker credentials or configurations.
findings.recommendation: Restrict SSH access to internal IPs or VPN only. Disable password authentication and enforce SSH key-based login.

findings.title: MySQL database port exposed on development host
findings.description: MySQL (port 3306) is directly accessible from the internet. This is a severe misconfiguration on any internet-facing host.
findings.recommendation: Immediately bind MySQL to 127.0.0.1. Remove external firewall access to port 3306.

findings.title: API key pattern found in public GitHub repository
findings.description: A search for 'example.com+api_key' returned a public repository containing what appears to be a live API key for example.com. Pattern: EXMPL_KEY_[A-Z0-9]{32}.
findings.recommendation: Immediately revoke the exposed key and rotate credentials. Audit git history for additional secrets and add pre-commit hooks to prevent future leaks.

findings.title: Cloudflare WAF detected
findings.description: api.example.com is protected by Cloudflare WAF. Adjust scanning rate limits and consider WAF bypass techniques if testing is authorized.
findings.recommendation: No remediation needed. Confirm WAF rules are configured to block common OWASP Top 10 attack patterns.

findings.title: Internal subdomain exposed in certificate transparency logs
findings.description: Certificate transparency logs reveal 'internal.example.com' which was not discovered by active enumeration. This host may expose internal tooling.
findings.recommendation: Audit what 'internal.example.com' serves. If it contains internal tools, restrict access to VPN/internal networks.
